libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Tests that exercise real requests should create an Akka ActorSystem and Materializer, instantiate a StandaloneAhcWSClient, and always close the client and terminate the ActorSystem. Prefer a local in-process JDK HttpServer or equivalent loopback server instead of external network calls. Avoid registering termination callbacks that call System.exit in tests.

deterministic_setup.reason:
Play WS documentation says standalone usage starts by adding play-ahc-ws-standalone, which provides StandaloneAhcWSClient/AhcWSClient and the shaded AsyncHttpClient implementation; the requested play-ws-standalone artifact mainly supplies the Java/Scala WS API, body read/write abstractions, request/
...[truncated]

risks: The companion AHC dependency broadens the runtime graph with shaded AsyncHttpClient/Netty classes, so generated tests should keep assertions focused on the requested play-ws-standalone APIs and use local verification as authoritative.
Optional JSON/XML body support is documented as separate add-ons, but it is not required for baseline meaningful coverage of the requested artifact.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: The requested artifact is the Play WS standalone API jar; normal documented use instantiates a concrete AHC-backed standalone client from the companion play-ahc-ws-standalone artifact before meaningful HTTP-client coverage is reachable.
